title: Implement hex parser + unit tests
description:
Implement `swatch_parse_hex` as declared in `src/parse.h` (produced by swatch-scaffold). This is a pure function with no I/O, no globals, no side effects outside the passed-in out pointer â€” the easiest possible target for unit testing and sanitizer coverage.

This task DEPENDS on swatch-scaffold being complete: parse.h, tests/test.h, and the Makefile `test` target must exist. Do NOT modify parse.h â€” the signature and contract are fixed.

Run in parallel with swatch-renderer; the two tasks share no source files.
requirements:
Implement `src/parse.c`:
  - `#include "parse.h"` and `<ctype.h>`, `<string.h>`.
  - Function `int swatch_parse_hex(const char *input, rgb_t *out)` matching the header contract.
  - Return -1 immediately if `input == NULL` or `out == NULL`.
  - Skip a single leading `#` if present.
  - After optional `#`, the remainder must be exactly 3 or 6 hex characters (no other lengths, no embedded `#`, no whitespace).
  - Reject on any non-hex character via `isxdigit((unsigned char)c)`. Use the `(unsigned char)` cast â€” isxdigit's behavior is undefined on signed char values outside [0, UCHAR_MAX].
  - For 3-char form, expand each nibble by duplication: `'A'` â†’ byte `0xAA`. For 6-char form, consume two hex digits per channel.
  - Parse hex digits manually (do NOT use `strtol` / `sscanf` â€” both complicate error handling for this contract). A small `hex_nibble(char) â†’ int` static helper returning -1 on non-hex is the cleanest shape.
  - Only assign to `*out` AFTER all validation has passed. On failure, `*out` must be untouched.

Implement `tests/test_parse.c`:
  - `#include "../src/parse.h"` and `#include "test.h"`.
  - Test functions (each `static void name(void)`):
    * `test_six_char_with_hash` â€” `#00AF4C` â†’ r=0x00 g=0xAF b=0x4C, return 0.
    * `test_six_char_without_hash` â€” `FF00FF` â†’ r=0xFF g=0x00 b=0xFF, return 0.
    * `test_three_char_with_hash` â€” `#0AF` â†’ r=0x00 g=0xAA b=0xFF, return 0.
    * `test_three_char_without_hash` â€” `abc` â†’ r=0xAA g=0xBB b=0xCC (lowercase accepted), return 0.
    * `test_uppercase_and_lowercase_mix` â€” `#aAfF00` â†’ r=0xAA g=0xFF b=0x00, return 0.
    * `test_reject_wrong_length` â€” `#12`, `#1234`, `#1234567` all return -1.
    * `test_reject_non_hex` â€” `#ZZZZZZ`, `#GGHHII`, `#00AF4G` all return -1.
    * `test_reject_null_input` â€” `swatch_parse_hex(NULL, &rgb)` returns -1.
    * `test_reject_null_output` â€” `swatch_parse_hex("#000000", NULL)` returns -1.
    * `test_reject_embedded_hash` â€” `00#F4C` returns -1.
    * `test_reject_whitespace` â€” `" #00AF4C"`, `"#00AF4C "`, `"00 AF4C"` all return -1.
    * `test_out_untouched_on_failure` â€” initialize out to known sentinel values, call with invalid input, assert all three channels still hold sentinels.
  - `int main(void)` invokes each via `RUN_TEST(...)` and ends with `TEST_SUMMARY()`.

Verify: `make test` exits 0 with ASan + UBSan enabled. `make clean && make` (release build) also exits 0 â€” proves parse.c compiles warning-free under `-Werror` in release mode too. Report the output of both.
